column2: A technician needs to change legacy internet security zones and certificate settings. Which Control Panel destination should the technician use?
column5: Internet Options groups legacy internet properties such as security, privacy, content certificates, connections, programs, and advanced settings. It is not the destination for configuring a network adapter itself.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 11
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings InternetOptions Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user wants a simple Control Panel view for adding and configuring a printer. Which destination best matches the task?
column5: Devices and Printers offers a simplified view of connected devices. Device Manager is the better match when the task concerns driver or hardware status.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 11
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Devices Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs to enable or disable an optional Windows component. Which Control Panel destination should the technician use?
column5: Programs and Features manages installed desktop applications and provides the path for turning optional Windows features on or off.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 11
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic HighYield WindowsSettings Applications Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician wants the Control Panel destination for viewing network adapters and changing adapter or sharing settings. Which destination should be opened?
column5: Network and Sharing Center collects adapter, addressing, and sharing configuration. Internet Options instead handles legacy internet properties.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 11
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Networking Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs to reach remote-access and system-protection settings from Control Panel. Which applet provides those paths?
column5: The System applet also presents computer and Windows information. Network and Sharing Center is for adapter and sharing configuration, not Remote Desktop or system protection.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 11
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic HighYield WindowsSettings System Source::Messer-v140

column2: A support task requires changing the local Windows host-firewall configuration. Which Control Panel destination should be used?
column5: Windows Defender Firewall is the operating system's local firewall interface. It is distinct from Network and Sharing Center, which manages network and sharing settings.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 11
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Firewall Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user with a compatible desktop mail client needs to manage a local mail profile and its data files. Which Control Panel applet should be used?
column5: The Mail applet appears when a supported mail client is installed and exposes local account, profile, and data-file configuration.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 11
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Mail Source::Messer-v140

column2: A PC has several speakers and microphones. Which Control Panel destination lets the user select devices and adjust input or output levels?
column5: Sound handles audio input and output devices and their levels. Device Manager would be used instead for an audio device's driver or hardware status.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 11
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Audio Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs to change a local account's type, password, or picture in Control Panel. Which applet should be opened?
column5: User Accounts manages local account details. Domain accounts are maintained in the domain rather than by this local Control Panel applet.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Accounts Source::Messer-v140

column2: A newly connected device is not working, and the technician needs immediate hardware status and driver-management options. Which tool should be opened?
column5: Device Manager is the direct destination for hardware and driver status and for actions such as enabling, disabling, adding, or removing a device.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic HighYield WindowsSettings Drivers Source::Messer-v140

column2: Windows searches need to include an additional folder that is not currently indexed. Which Control Panel destination should the technician modify?
column5: Indexing Options controls which locations Windows indexes to speed searches. File Explorer search options influence search behavior but do not replace the index-location configuration.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Search Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician must access a folder that File Explorer does not normally display. Which settings destination contains the option to show hidden files?
column4: File Explorer Options, under the View settings.
column5: The View settings control whether hidden content appears. This is separate from showing file extensions, even though both choices are in File Explorer Options.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic HighYield WindowsSettings FileExplorer Source::Messer-v140

column2: Two files have similar names and icons, so a technician needs to see their actual filename suffixes. Which File Explorer setting should be changed?
column4: Disable the option that hides extensions for known file types.
column5: Showing extensions exposes the suffix used to identify the file type. Showing hidden files solves a different visibility problem.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic HighYield WindowsSettings FileExplorer Source::Messer-v140

column2: A laptop must preserve open work on disk before powering down so the session does not depend on continuous memory power. Which power state should be used?
column5: Hibernate saves the session to disk. Sleep keeps the active session in memory for a quicker return but continues to require some power.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Power Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user wants a reduced-power state that keeps open applications in memory for a quick return. Which power state fits the goal?
column5: Sleep retains the working session in memory. Hibernate writes the session to disk and is the better choice when maintaining memory power is undesirable.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Power Source::Messer-v140

column2: An administrator wants to switch a laptop between predefined groups of energy and performance behavior. Which Power Options feature should be configured?
column5: A power plan groups power-usage settings so the system can be tuned for different energy or performance goals.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Power Source::Messer-v140

column2: A docked laptop should keep running when its built-in display is closed. Which Power Options setting should be changed?
column4: Choose what closing the lid does, and set the applicable lid-close action to keep the system running.
column5: The configurable lid-close action is useful at a docking station, where the laptop may need to keep operating with external devices in use.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic HighYield WindowsSettings Power Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician is investigating startup behavior and wants to test whether a configurable power feature is contributing. Which Power Options feature can be disabled during this troubleshooting?
column5: Fast Startup uses hibernation and can be enabled or disabled while troubleshooting startup behavior.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic HighYield WindowsSettings Startup Source::Messer-v140

column2: A laptop should reduce power use by suspending individual idle USB devices. Which advanced power feature provides this behavior?
column5: Selective suspend targets individual USB devices instead of putting the whole computer into sleep or hibernation.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings USB Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user needs Windows usability changes for the display, keyboard, and mouse. Which Control Panel destination groups these accessibility settings?
column5: Ease of Access groups interaction and accessibility adjustments, including options for users who need alternate display, pointer, keyboard, or screen-free operation.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Accessibility Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user needs to add a Windows display language and correct automatic clock configuration. Which Windows Settings category contains these options?
column5: Time and Language groups clock, regional, and language configuration. Correct time is also important for environments that rely on synchronized authentication.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Localization Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user wants Windows Update to avoid restarting the PC during normal working hours. Which Settings category contains the active-hours configuration?
column5: Update and Security manages operating-system updates. Active hours identify the normal use period so update restarts are less disruptive.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Updates Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user wants to change the desktop wallpaper, colors, and lock-screen appearance. Which Windows Settings category should be used?
column5: Personalization changes how Windows looks and feels. It does not manage user identity or sign-in methods; those belong under Accounts.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Personalization Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user wants the modern Windows Settings category for uninstalling an installed application. Which category should be opened?
column5: Apps manages installed applications and optional features in Windows Settings. Programs and Features is the related legacy Control Panel destination.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Applications Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user wants to review whether Windows shares activity, language, or speech data with online services. Which Windows Settings category should be opened?
column5: Privacy contains controls for information and activity shared by applications or services. Accounts instead manages identity and sign-in configuration.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 12
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Privacy Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user needs to change display scaling, Night light, and notification behavior in Windows Settings. Which category contains these options?
column5: The System category covers display, audio, and notification settings. The legacy System Control Panel applet instead emphasizes computer information and paths to remote and protection settings.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 13
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings System Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user wants the Windows Settings category for Bluetooth devices, printers, mouse behavior, and pen input. Which category should be opened?
column5: Devices groups peripheral and input configuration in Windows Settings. Device Manager remains the better tool for hardware status and drivers.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 13
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Devices Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs the Windows Settings category that shows connection status and provides network and IP configuration. Which category should be opened?
column5: Network and Internet is the modern Settings destination for connectivity and address configuration. Network and Sharing Center is the related Control Panel destination.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 13
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic HighYield WindowsSettings Networking Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user needs to configure Xbox Game Bar and related Windows gaming features. Which Windows Settings category should be used?
column5: Gaming groups Xbox Game Bar and related gaming-network features. It is not the destination for graphics display scaling or general notifications.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 13
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Gaming Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user wants to change between local and Microsoft account information and configure a PIN or security key. Which Windows Settings category should be opened?
column5: Accounts manages sign-in identity, email integration, and sign-in methods. Personalization changes appearance rather than authentication choices.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.6; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 13
column10: A+::220-1202::1.6 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsSettings Basic WindowsSettings Accounts Source::Messer-v140
